gists.title: Claude Flow Playbook for Advanced Coordination, Context Engineering, and Artifact-Centric Swarms
gists.purpose: Orchestration framework combining multi-agent swarms, SQLite-based memory persistence, and MCP tool integration for scaling intelligent systems beyond token management

operational_modes.name: Swarm Mode
operational_modes.description: Quick ad-hoc tasks with minimal setup overhead

operational_modes.name: Hive-Mind Mode
operational_modes.description: Persistent multi-agent projects with resumption capability
operational_modes.use_case: Long-horizon complex projects requiring state persistence

coordination_mechanisms.name: Blackboard Pattern
coordination_mechanisms.description: Agents write hints to shared state and append actions to event logs

coordination_mechanisms.name: Consensus Gating
coordination_mechanisms.description: Critical transitions require recorded votes before execution

coordination_mechanisms.name: Event-Driven Audit
coordination_mechanisms.description: Every state change logged for deterministic replay

artifact_first_workflow: Reference by manifest ID across agents
Avoid rehydrating large text in prompts

goap.name: Goal-Oriented Action Planning
features: Use A* planning to sequence execution

phases.decide: Write candidate to consensus_state, gate on votes

performance_optimization: Session Forking - spawn agents rapidly without sequential overhead
Hook Matchers - execute only necessary pre/post-processing
In-Process MCP - near-zero IPC latency for local tools
WAL Mode - write-ahead logging for concurrent database reads
Batch writes in transactions during intensive phases
topology_modes: Hierarchical - coordinator delegates to specialized sub-agents

standards_and_protocols: Model Context Protocol (MCP) - tool discovery and invocation
SQLite with WAL - concurrent read capability

architectural_patterns.name: Coordinator with Bounded Context
architectural_patterns.description: Coordinator reads top-K bundle only, delegates to role-specific agents
architectural_patterns.benefits: Reduced context overhead, clear separation of concerns

architectural_patterns.name: Adaptive Topology
architectural_patterns.description: Initialize hierarchical, switch to mesh under parallelism, fallback to ring/star on contention

architectural_patterns.name: Artifact-Manifest Decoupling
architectural_patterns.benefits: Efficient reference passing, reduced context rehydration

tracked_metrics: Context bundle size and artifact hit rate
Pattern reuse rate and defect escape rate
five_dimension_scorecard: Context Efficiency - bundle size vs. quality
Learning - pattern reuse and defect correlation

features: Tool access control at enterprise scope

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Use blackboard pattern for coordinating 19+ AQE agents in complex test scenarios
examples: Coordinate functional, security, and performance agents
Share test data and results across agent boundaries
Implement consensus gating for critical test decisions

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Store test execution history, learned patterns, and API specifications in SQLite
examples: Persist test results and coverage data
Store learned test patterns for reuse
Cache API specifications and schemas

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Optimize context bundles for test generation and execution
examples: Inject only relevant API specs during test generation
Limit context to top-5 relevant test patterns
Reduce token usage in multi-agent test workflows

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Enable long-running test campaigns to pause and resume
examples: Resume failed test runs from checkpoint
Pause performance tests and continue later
Maintain state across distributed test executions

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Complete audit trail for compliance and debugging
examples: Track all test decisions and results
Replay test executions for debugging
Provide evidence for compliance audits

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Dynamic agent topology based on test workload
examples: Hierarchical for complex API testing
Mesh for parallel test execution
Ring for sequential integration tests

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Goal-oriented test planning with A* search
examples: Plan optimal test execution sequence
Identify dependencies between test cases
Optimize test coverage with minimal redundancy

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Track and optimize test execution performance
examples: Monitor test execution latency
Track agent performance metrics
Identify bottlenecks in test workflows

components.notes: Already using PostgreSQL, need to integrate SQLite for agent memory
Can implement using existing Redis or new SQLite tables
New voting mechanism needed for multi-agent decisions
Integrate with existing agent execution pipeline
Requires A* implementation and state modeling

expected_benefits.mechanism: Context engineering reduces token usage through targeted artifact retrieval
GOAP planning and memory persistence improve task success
Parallel agent execution with adaptive topology
Event-driven logging for compliance and debugging

gists.title: ReasoningBank: Algorithmic Memory System for Claude Flow
gists.purpose: Enable agents to learn from task trajectories through strategic memory creation, retrieval, and consolidation. Implements continuous self-improvement from both successes and failures.

tables.name: pattern_embeddings
tables.description: Stores vector representations for semantic retrieval

tables.name: pattern_links
tables.description: Tracks relationships between memories (deduplication, contradictions, refinements)
tables.purpose: Memory graph maintenance and quality control

tables.name: task_trajectories
tables.description: Archives complete execution paths with success/failure labels

components: Content - 3-8 numbered procedural steps with decision criteria
Metadata - confidence scores, usage counts, domain tags, temporal tracking
memory_item_structure.example_format:
Strategy memories you can optionally use.
1) [Title] Avoid infinite pagination loops
   Steps: Detect repeated DOM states...

retrieval.deduplication: Maximal Marginal Relevance to prevent near-duplicate selections

judgment.output: Success or Failure with confidence scores
judgment.integration: Feeds into task_trajectories and event logs

distillation.success_path: Extracts reusable strategic principles, avoiding task-specific constants and PII
distillation.failure_path: Generates preventative guardrails and recovery procedures
distillation.output: Up to m items per trajectory through structured prompting

parallel_mode.description: Launch k independent rollouts with diversity seeds

steps: Collect intermediate signals across refinement steps

system_prompt_injection.format: Numbered items with title and steps
system_prompt_injection.example:
Strategy memories you can optionally use.
1) [Title] Avoid infinite pagination loops
   Steps: Detect repeated DOM states...

learning_mechanisms.feedback_loop: Closed-loop self-improvement from both successes and failures

innovative_approaches.name: Closed-loop self-improvement
innovative_approaches.description: Learn from task failures, not just successes
innovative_approaches.innovation: Most systems only learn from positive examples; this learns from failures too

innovative_approaches.name: MaTTS orchestration
innovative_approaches.description: Convert inference compute into persistent memory capital
innovative_approaches.innovation: Test-time scaling that produces reusable knowledge assets

innovative_approaches.name: Natural integration
innovative_approaches.description: Seamless integration with Claude Flow's existing memory primitives
innovative_approaches.innovation: Extends rather than replaces existing infrastructure

innovative_approaches.name: Governance layer
innovative_approaches.description: Deduplication and contradiction checks prevent memory corruption
innovative_approaches.innovation: Maintains memory quality over time through active curation

innovative_approaches.name: Strategic principle extraction
innovative_approaches.description: Avoid task-specific constants, extract generalizable patterns
innovative_approaches.innovation: Focus on transferable knowledge rather than instance-specific details

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Learn effective test patterns from successful and failed test executions
examples: Extract patterns from successful API test cases
Learn from failed security tests to prevent future issues
Build library of reusable test strategies
Identify common API testing pitfalls

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Generate better tests over time based on learned patterns
examples: Inject learned patterns into test generation prompts
Avoid known anti-patterns in generated tests
Prioritize high-value test scenarios
Optimize test coverage based on historical effectiveness

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Build guardrails from past test failures
examples: Prevent infinite loops in test execution
Avoid rate limiting issues
Handle edge cases discovered in past runs
Implement recovery procedures for known failure modes

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Use test-time scaling for high-stakes security and performance tests
examples: Run k parallel security test variations and extract best patterns
Iteratively refine performance test scenarios
Generate comprehensive edge case coverage
Build high-confidence test suites through parallel exploration

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Retrieve relevant test patterns during execution
examples: Inject top-3 relevant patterns before test generation
Use semantic similarity to find applicable strategies
Apply recency weighting for recent API changes
Balance reliability and diversity in pattern selection

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Build and refine understanding of API behavior over time
examples: Learn API quirks and undocumented behavior
Track API changes and versioning patterns
Build confidence in endpoint behavior
Detect breaking changes through pattern drift
Identify optimal agent handoff patterns
Learn when to use parallel vs sequential execution
Extract successful collaboration patterns
Optimize agent topology based on task type

potential_sentinel_applications.description: Learn effective test data patterns from successful tests
examples: Extract boundary value patterns
Learn schema-specific data generation strategies
Identify effective edge case patterns
Build corpus of realistic test data

components.notes: Add 4 new tables to existing SQLite/PostgreSQL
Integrate embedding service and vector storage
Implement scoring formula and MMR deduplication
Integrate with existing agent execution hooks

expected_benefits.mechanism: Learn from every test execution, both success and failure
expected_benefits.quantification: 20-40% improvement in test effectiveness over baseline

expected_benefits.mechanism: Inject learned patterns to avoid known pitfalls
expected_benefits.quantification: 30-50% reduction in failed test generation attempts

expected_benefits.mechanism: Reuse proven patterns rather than start from scratch
expected_benefits.quantification: 2-3x faster test generation for similar APIs

expected_benefits.mechanism: MaTTS parallel exploration discovers comprehensive test scenarios
Continuous learning tracks evolving API behavior
expected_benefits.quantification: Maintain 85%+ test accuracy despite API evolution

expected_benefits.mechanism: Strategic principles apply to similar API patterns
expected_benefits.quantification: 40-60% faster testing for new APIs in same domain

expected_benefits.mechanism: Learned guardrails prevent common failure modes

high_priority_integrations.rationale: Self-improving test generation aligned with Sentinel's AI-first approach
high_priority_integrations.expected_roi: 2-3x improvement in test quality over 6 months

high_priority_integrations.rationale: Natural fit for coordinating 19+ AQE agents
Critical for compliance and debugging in enterprise testing
Immediate 30%+ token savings on test generation

low_priority_integrations.rationale: Visibility into agent and test performance

test_quality.measurement: Bug detection rate, false positive rate, coverage completeness

cost_efficiency.baseline: Current token usage and LLM costs
cost_efficiency.measurement: Tokens per test, cost per test suite

development_velocity.measurement: Time to generate test suite, manual intervention needed

system_reliability.baseline: Current test failure and retry rates
system_reliability.measurement: Failed generation attempts, manual fixes needed

synergy_opportunities.description: Use PostgreSQL for persistent data, SQLite for agent memory
synergy_opportunities.benefit: Best of both worlds - scalability and fast agent coordination

synergy_opportunities.description: Combine ruv-swarm (Rust) with Claude Flow (TypeScript) orchestration
synergy_opportunities.benefit: Performance where needed, flexibility for complex coordination

synergy_opportunities.description: Apply ReasoningBank learning across multiple LLM providers
synergy_opportunities.benefit: Learn optimal provider selection patterns for different task types

synergy_opportunities.description: Integrate event logging with existing message broker
synergy_opportunities.benefit: Unified event processing pipeline, better scalability

synergy_opportunities.description: Coordinate 19 AQE agents via blackboard pattern
synergy_opportunities.benefit: Reduced coupling, better parallelism, clearer handoffs

synergy_opportunities.description: Store ReasoningBank embeddings in PostgreSQL pgvector
